summary: TypeDoc pilot for TS. Parallel surface produced. Key finding: TypeDoc catches 2 new functions + filters 33 @hidden internals. Structural gap: declare global namespace Amity{} (946 members) not captured. Recommendation: hybrid merger (0082) rather than direct swap.

changes.description: TypeDoc-based TS extractor. Reads /tmp/typedoc-output.json (from src/index.ts with --skipErrorChecking --excludeExternals). Produces same schema shape as typescript.json. TypeDoc invocation that works: packages/sdk/ entry, src/index.ts, --tsconfig tsconfig.json.
Parallel TypeDoc surface. namespaces=24 root_exports=176 namespaced_members=381 amity_global_members=0 (gap). Parallel only — typescript.json unchanged.

6-section comparison. Section 2: TypeDoc wins (getCommunityByIds, getSubChannelByIds, 5 enum cases). Section 3: regex-only root causes (946 Amity global gap + 33 @hidden false positives). Section 5: coverage analysis. Section 6: hold + hybrid merger recommendation (task 0082).
Added typescript-typedoc-extractor.py to table. Updated Android status to complete. Added TS migration pilot section with key findings.

notes: TypeDoc invocation: src/index.ts against packages/sdk/tsconfig.json --skipErrorChecking --excludeExternals. dist/index.d.ts approach failed. Source-based: 2.4MB JSON in ~10s. The declare global namespace Amity{} gap is the decisive blocker for a direct swap — it needs a secondary @types/ scan or explicit export to resolve. Unlike iOS/Android where new source was strictly better, TS needs a hybrid approach. TypeDoc's @hidden filtering is a genuine accuracy win: 33 internal symbols currently over-captured by regex.
